item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This one doesn't seem too bad, it was a fun read. I like it, upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…a 67 year old mute and deaf man being treated by Doctor ████ who was, according to staff at the Asylum, portrayed his emotions…

who was portrayed his emotions? huh?

item.title: Really cool!
item.content:encoded: This is actually really cool and interesting, and I think it deserves more praise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Something that's bothering me:

Doctor ████: Hello Patient #████.

The Foundation may refer to people by number but a mental health institution would use the guy's name.

item.title: Re: Classification
item.content:encoded:
It has sapience but no agency (it can't move).
It also can be contained- it replicates itself, but the previously produced pages can be destroyed once they've been used up. The Foundation just saves them because they make a handy record of the SCP's behavior.

item.title: Classification
item.content:encoded: Wouldn't this object, by nature of its apparent sapience and theoretical inability to contain be listed as Euclid?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Same here, and we have a living drawing the communicates, and a pencil that communicates. This isn't different enough from many of what we have already to be interesting. -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eh, this is a story about a drawing that communicates. We already have one that I can think of off the top of my head, and doubtlessly multiple others that I can't. This plays the tired concept pretty much exactly straight, with the exception of some logs that add nothing but length.

There are some attempts at feels stapled on for no apparent reason. In the best case, feels can add emotional depth and even recontextualize an article; this just goes for "the object is sad"-style pathos, without giving the reader any reason to care.

-1. I would suggest running this by the forum/chat again. One angle I wish you had gone with was the fact that the "creator" of the object was in an asylum. While it's not a good idea to just go with "he was crazy and so weirdness," there is plenty of stuff to explore if you chose to go with a nuanced and well-researched portrayal of mental illness and/or dementia

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow, thanks, I must've missed that!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-XXXX, photo taken from Researcher Darwin

shouldn't it be

SCP-3289, photo taken from Researcher Darwin

?

item.title: Image Source
item.content:encoded:
Image created by a person I met in a Discord who requested to be credited as "Researcher Darwin"

Conversation from Discord:

https://ibb.co/kEcs5Q
https://ibb.co/icu5QQ
https://ibb.co/iN6fs5

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Sent in PM.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Have you got any screenshots or such of that?

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image I used here was made for me by someone I met in a Discord. I asked him how he wanted to be credited and he said that he wanted to be cited as "Researcher Darwin", so that's what I put. If this is a problem, please notify me.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Images used in posted articles must comply with the site's image policy. A source must be provided on the discussion page and the source must be compatible with the site's license. The author will be notified after this posting via Wikidot PM and will have 48 hours to properly source the image before it is edited out of the page.

EDIT: Image has been removed due to a lack of proof of source, however, if rectified it may be added back in. Author has been notified.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The votes seem fairly divided now. If it gets deleted, make a thread and PM me. I'll make a point of it to review this line-by-line.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In your opinion, should I take down this and put it back up on the forums?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In the future, make sure that at least a few members of the wiki (especially established authors) will say that it's ready. PM me or another member of the Forum Crit team if you struggle to find people to review it. Alternatively, ask the critters on the SCP Wiki Chat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm fairly certain they were, since they made a Discord server dedicated to SCP. While I'm not sure if the server is official (I doubt it is), they looked to know what they were doing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I see. Were they members of the wiki itself? Reviewers?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I got reviewed by others in a Discord, who looked it over and gave me advice. They said they thought it was ready.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Two people reviewed your draft. One of them was called out by an administrator because their critique was misleading. On top of that, none of those two actually said that it was ready.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-3289 then draws various dog faces, contorted into smiles, for the remaining time.

Something like that? (I added that in.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The description mentions a variety of faces but the test logs only talk about the patient's face being drawn. This could be fixed by having SCP-3289 draw dogs that it sees after it reacts with the realistic face.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If you think there's anything I need to fix, please tell me here.

Side note: I did get this reviewed by multiple people, and they told me that they thought this was ready to be put up. If you can tell me why you disliked it, please do.
